channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: It’s not easy, surrogate mother shares emotional journey
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Nigerian surrogate mother shares her emotional journey, detailing financial hardship, the loss of a child, and the psychological realities of surrogacy.
Read More: https://punchng.com/how-poverty-irresponsible-partner-pushed-me-into-surrogacy-woman/

item.title: Step-by-step guide for contactless passport renewal for Nigerians abroad
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerians abroad can now renew their passports contactlessly. Follow this step-by-step guide for a seamless application process with the NIS.
Read More: https://punchng.com/step-by-step-guide-for-contactless-passport-renewal-for-nigerians-abroad/

item.title: Hoodlums tie up couple, steal ₦4m valuables in Bauchi – Police
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Bauchi Police have arrested two suspects involved in a N4m robbery where hoodlums tied up a couple. Efforts are ongoing to apprehend fleeing suspects.
Read More: https://punchng.com/hoodlums-tie-up-couple-steal-%e2%82%a64m-valuables-in-bauchi-police/

item.title: Rivers ADC nominates Okumgba as deputy governorship candidate
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The African Democratic Congress (ADC) in Rivers State has nominated David Okumgba as its deputy governorship candidate for the 2027 elections.
Read More: https://punchng.com/rivers-adc-nominates-okumgba-as-deputy-governorship-candidate/

item.title: VIDEO: Lagos to profile, repatriate 396 beggars arrested in sanitation sweep
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lagos State Government has arrested 396 beggars in a sanitation sweep. They will be profiled, assisted, and repatriated to their home states. Learn more ab
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-lagos-to-profile-repatriate-396-beggars-arrested-in-sanitation-sweep/

item.title: 2027: Ogundipe clears air on Ogun ADC governorship ticket

item.description:
Biodun Ogundipe confirms he is the recognised Ogun ADC governorship candidate for 2027, addressing controversies and outlining his tech-driven agenda for t
Read More: https://punchng.com/2027-ogundipe-clears-air-on-ogun-adc-governorship-ticket/

item.title: US Consulate, FBI seek stronger EFCC ties on sextortion
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The US Consulate and FBI are strengthening ties with EFCC to combat sextortion and transnational financial crimes. Learn about their collaborative efforts.
Read More: https://punchng.com/us-consulate-fbi-seek-stronger-efcc-ties-on-sextortion/

item.title: Police detain officers over assault of civilian in Ebonyi
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Ebonyi State Police Command detains officers involved in civilian assault, launching a thorough investigation into the alleged police brutality incident.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-detain-officers-over-assault-of-civilian-in-ebonyi/

item.title: FG mourns medical graduate killed in Ukraine attack
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Federal Government expresses deep sorrow over the death of a Nigerian medical graduate, Nnani Adaobi Marian, killed in a Russian attack in Ukraine.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-mourns-medical-graduate-killed-in-ukraine-attack/

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: She explained that women should be accorded the same grace and mercy accorded to men.

item.title: The Nasdaq-100 has been far more volatile than the S&P 500. Now add SpaceX to the mix.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: While SpaceX is due to join the Nasdaq-100 on Tuesday, it won’t become part of the S&P 500 for at least another year — likely furthering the volatility spread between those two indexes.

item.title: Egypt: Hossam Hassan to Maintain Egypt's Identity Against Star-Studded World Champions Argentina
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [CAF] Egypt head coach Hossam Hassan says his side will remain true to its identity when it takes on the reigning world champions, Argentina in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 on Tuesday evening, insisting his players will focus on their own game rather than the stature of their star-studded opponent

item.description: According to the ministry, Marian “reportedly succumbed to injuries sustained during a Russian aerial attack in Kharkiv, Ukraine.” The post FG mourns Nigerian medical graduate killed in Russian airstrike appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Nigeria pauses new Internet platform rules pending unified digital policy
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: The Nigerian government has halted the enforcement of overlapping rules for internet platforms, marking its strongest move yet to create a single regulatory framework for the digital economy.

item.title: Chinese ex-official sentenced to death over $325 million bribery scheme
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A Chinese court has sentenced former Nanjing official Yang Youlin to death after finding him guilty of accepting more than 2.2 billion yuan (about $325 million) in bribes over a period spanning three decades. item.title: Fincantieri Says Hybrid Warfare Is Growing Threat to Europe Seas

item.title: Syria: Macron ‘safe’ after blasts near French president’s hotel
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Two bomb explosions rocked Syria’s Damascus on Tuesday, close to the hotel where French President Emmanuel Macron was staying as he visited the country. The Élysée palace said Macron was safe, with his motorcade seen leaving the hotel before the explosions.

item.title: PFIPC: Tinubu either absentee president or accomplice – Atiku on allegations against Gbajabiamila
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The presidential candidate of the African Democratic Congress and former Vice President, Abubakar Atiku, has alleged that President Bola Ahmed Tinubu is either an absentee leader or an accomplice over the controversies surrounding the Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council. He disc

item.title: Iraq Boosts Oil Output at Three Southern Fields to Full Capacity
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Iraq has raised production from three key projects in the country’s oil heartland to full capacity as tankers have started arriving to export cargoes, a person familiar with the operations said.

item.title: Relief as abducted UI law student, others regain freedom
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: There was relief across the University of Ibadan community on Monday as Miss Fumilayo, a 300-level Law student who was abducted alongside other individuals, regained her freedom. The University of Ibadan Students’ Union confirmed the development in a statement signed by its President, Temidayo

item.title: Apologise or face legal action – Paraguayan senator to Mbappe
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Paraguayan Senator Celeste Amarilla has demanded a public apology from France captain Kylian Mbappe, warning that she could institute legal proceedings if he fails to withdraw remarks describing her as "despicable" following their World Cup row. The post Apologise or face legal action – Paraguayan s

item.title: These global stocks benefit from high barriers to entry. And they’re winning the war against AI disruption.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Goldman Sachs says the HALO trade has worked well this year, but a tougher phase relying on earnings performance lies ahead.

item.title: German Gen Z joins the Bundeswehr: The military becomes a plan B
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Germany is rearming amid an economic recession with thousands of jobs slashed across industrial and service sectors. In these tough economic conditions, the Bundeswehr is now one of the country's most attractive employers for young people. Applications rose by 23 percent in January 2026 alone. Behin

item.title: Demand for state police due to federal police failure — Ex-IGP Okiro
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "If the police had been well-equipped, nobody would be talking about state police. People are talking about state police because the federal police have failed. If they had succeeded, nobody would talk about it." The post Demand for state police due to federal police failure — Ex-IGP Okiro appeared

item.title: After 18 years, Nollywood star Ricardo Agbor welcomes twins
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actor Ricardo Agbor has shared details of his 18-year journey to parenthood, revealing that he and his wife waited nearly two decades before welcoming their twins The post After 18 years, Nollywood star Ricardo Agbor welcomes twins appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Lagos sweeps up 396 beggars in sanitation crackdown
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The commissioner said the exercise reflected the government's determination to uphold public order, safeguard the environment and make communities safer.

item.title: How Major Sports Leagues Shape Online Betting Markets
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Elite sports leagues consistently drive both digital excitement and real-time wagering activity, shaping the rhythm of betting markets around the world. High-profile tournaments and iconic fixtures naturally boost engagement, influencing both casual bettors and industry operators alike. With digital

item.title: What to know about Macron's landmark visit to Syria
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron's visit to Syria is historic: he is the first EU leader to visit the country since the rebels took over and ousted dictator al-Assad. Both Macron and al-Sharaa hope to build a path towards Syria's rehabilitation, but two unclaimed explosions in Damascus near Macron's

item.title: ‎All three co-hosts eliminated from 2026 World Cup
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The three countries hosting the 2026 FIFA World Cup have all been eliminated before the quarter-final stage, bringing an early end to the tournament hopes of the United States, Mexico and Canada. The United States became the final co-host to exit the competition after suffering a heavy 4-1 defeat to

item.title: At the World Cup, Belgium knock out US team tainted by red card controversy
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Donald Trump intervention wasn’t enough. Belgium have beaten and knocked the United States out of the World Cup. A 4-1 victory for the Red Devils in a match where all eyes were on Florian Balogun, the US striker who had been shown a red card and was not due to play until FIFA lifted his suspension f

item.title: Peter Obi can’t face me, let alone Tinubu — Umahi
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “Look at the pattern of politics around Peter Obi. You disagree with him, then he takes you to court. How many times have you been abusing President Bola Ahmed Tinubu? How many people has he taken to court?” he said.

item.title: A 24% boost to your Social Security check could be yours — if you're willing to wait 3 extra years
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Sheriff Blasts Obi Over Tinubu Resignation Call, Alleges He Is Pursuing Nigeria’s Breakup
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Borno State governor, Ali Modu Sheriff, has launched a scathing attack on the Presidential Candidate of Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, , Peter Obi, declaring that he lacks the moral authority to demand the resignation of President Bola Ahmed Tinubu.   Sheriff accused the forme
